description: Conversational mixed-input survey flow for DPI validation and DCLO capability outcomes

questions.prompt: Before we begin, do you consent to participate? You can say yes or no, or press 1 for yes and 2 for no.
What best describes your role? For example policy maker, researcher, NGO manager, entrepreneur, or student. You can say it in your own words, or press 1 policy, 2 research, 3 program, 4 other.

What kind of organization are you closest to? For example government, academia, NGO, private company, or independent. You can say it directly, or press 1 government, 2 academia, 3 NGO, 4 private, 5 other.
Which country are you responding from today? You can simply say the country name.
Were you able to complete your dashboard task today? You can say yes, partly, or no. Or press 1 yes, 2 partly, 3 no.
How useful was this survey and dashboard context for your real work? You can say a quick reason like very useful because it helps compare countries, or press a score from 1 to 5.
How easy was this experience? You can say something like smooth and clear or confusing and slow, or press 1 to 5.
How easy was it to interpret the output? You can describe briefly in your own words, or press 1 to 5.
How much do you trust these results for decision making? You can say a reason, for example data feels reliable or unclear, or press 1 to 5.
How actionable are these insights for policy decisions? For example can this support budget choices, targeting, or program design? You can speak freely or press 1 to 5.
Would you recommend this approach to colleagues? You can say yes strongly, maybe, or no and why, or press 1 to 5.
In your day to day life, how reliable is mobile internet when you need it for important tasks? You can give an example like payments, classes, or health services, or press 1 to 5.
How confident are you in completing digital tasks yourself, such as filling forms or using government apps? You can answer in words, or press 1 to 5.
How often do you use digital payments in daily life, like UPI, cards, or wallet apps? You can describe your pattern or press 1 to 5.
How often do you complete public service tasks digitally, for example certificates, registrations, or support requests? You can respond in words or press 1 to 5.
Do you feel in control of your own phone and digital accounts, including passwords and OTPs? You can explain briefly or press 1 to 5.
Overall, has digital access improved your quality of life in the last year, for example by saving time, money, or stress? You can answer freely or press 1 to 5.
